title: Best VPN for iPhone in 2026: Top iOS VPN Apps for Privacy, Speed, and Streaming
description: The best VPN apps for iPhone in 2026: which iOS VPNs are fastest, most private, and work reliably for streaming Netflix and other services on iOS.
content:
iPhone users face one challenge that Android users don't: Apple's tighter control over how VPN apps work on iOS. Understanding those limitations helps you pick the right VPN for your iPhone in 2026.

iOS VPN Limitations You Need to Know

All VPN apps on iPhone use Apple's NetworkExtension framework -- the same underlying API. This creates one important limitation: iOS does not support a true kill switch the same way Android does. On Android, a kill switch can block all traffic at the OS level if the VPN drops. On iOS, there is a partial implementation ("Always On VPN" mode) that works differently and requires MDM (Mobile Device Management) configuration in enterprise environments. For consumer VPN use on iPhone, if the VPN connection drops, your traffic may briefly revert to your regular connection before the app reconnects. This is a limitation of iOS, not the specific VPN app. Most VPN providers acknowledge this in their iOS documentation.

ExpressVPN for iPhone: Best Overall

ExpressVPN's iOS app is consistently ranked among the most polished VPN experiences for iPhone. The Lightway protocol (ExpressVPN's own protocol, based on WolfSSL) is designed for fast connection establishment and low battery drain on mobile -- important for an always-on VPN. The app supports split tunneling on iOS (exclude specific apps from VPN routing), connects automatically on untrusted Wi-Fi, and reliably unblocks Netflix US, BBC iPlayer, Disney+, and most streaming services. The shortcoming: one of the more expensive options ($8-10/month on annual plans).

NordVPN for iPhone: Close Second

NordVPN's iOS app uses NordLynx (WireGuard implementation), which matches ExpressVPN's Lightway in speed benchmarks. The app includes Threat Protection Lite (DNS-level ad and malware blocking) and auto-connect on untrusted networks. NordVPN's Threat Protection Lite works system-wide on iPhone, blocking ads and trackers across all apps, not just the browser -- a useful feature that goes beyond the core VPN functionality. Pricing is slightly lower than ExpressVPN on long-term plans.

Mullvad for iPhone: Best for Privacy

Mullvad requires no account creation -- you pay with a random account number. No email, no name, no personal data is collected. The iOS app is open source and has been audited by independent security researchers. Mullvad uses WireGuard on iOS. The trade-off: it does not prioritize streaming unblocking, so Netflix US and BBC iPlayer work inconsistently. If your primary goal is privacy rather than streaming access, Mullvad is the correct choice.

Proton VPN Free Tier: Only Trustworthy Free Option

If you want a free VPN for iPhone, Proton VPN is the only option worth recommending. No data selling, no ads, no artificial bandwidth cap. The free tier limits you to servers in Switzerland, the Netherlands, and the US, with lower speeds than paid tiers. For basic privacy protection on public Wi-Fi at no cost, it works. Avoid all other free VPN apps on the App Store -- the business model for free VPNs is almost always data monetization, which defeats the purpose.

Setup Tips for iPhone

After installing your VPN: enable auto-connect on untrusted Wi-Fi networks in the app settings. Set your preferred protocol to WireGuard or the provider's proprietary protocol (Lightway for ExpressVPN). Configure split tunneling if available -- exclude your banking app and other apps that block VPN connections. Enable the VPN's DNS leak protection if available in settings. For iPhone users on iOS 16+: check that your VPN app has network extension permissions in Settings, General, VPN and Device Management to ensure it can maintain the connection in the background.
